question: Can a different batched expert-Muon chunk size reduce the remaining optimizer-step wall?

runtime_probe.torch_optim_muon_impl_read: The installed torch.optim.Muon is single-tensor only; its functional API raises if foreach=True and calls _single_tensor_muon, which runs Newton-Schulz matmuls one 2D parameter at a time.

command_shape.base_command: docker run --rm --entrypoint /bin/bash --device=/dev/kfd --device=/dev/dri --group-add video --ipc=host --network=host --security-opt seccomp=unconfined -v /scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527:/workspace -w /workspace -e HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 -e PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True -e TORCHTITAN_OPTIMIZER_MUON_NS_COMPUTE_DTYPE=bfloat16 -e PYTHONPATH=/workspace/sources/wip/torchtitan onenexus/nexus-titan:rocm722-pytorch-nightly-mori -lc 'python3 /workspace/tools/dsv4_optimizer_preflight/probe_muon_optimizer_step.py --layers 12 --modes muon_batched --muon-ns-steps 1 --repeat 2'

read: The current chunk4 default is already the best measured NS1 point on the 12-layer expert-bank slice. Larger chunks do not improve speed and raise peak memory; smaller chunks are slower from extra loop/launch overhead. Combined with the runtime source check showing torch.optim.Muon has no foreach/fused path, this closes the easy Muon optimizer-step tuning branch.
decision: Do not run another e2e Muon chunk-size candidate. Keep expert-Muon batched chunk4 as the diagnostic memory branch and return optimization focus to MoE/EP helper VJP, weighted combine/scatter, and C4 sparse-MLA SharedKV backward.
